sources.title: Figma's Journey to Rust in Production
Scaling Figma's Rendering Engine with WebGL and WASM

result.summary: Figma evolved from a C++ to WebAssembly rendering engine with a Rust-based multiplayer backend, leveraging CRDTs for real-time collaboration and achieving sub-16ms frame times in browser through custom GPU-accelerated rendering pipelines.
key_findings: Core rendering engine compiled to WebAssembly from C++ achieves near-native performance with a custom 2D GPU renderer bypassing DOM entirely
Multiplayer collaboration built on CRDTs processed by a Rust server, replacing earlier OT-based approach for better offline and conflict resolution
Infrastructure migrated from AWS to a hybrid multi-cloud setup with edge nodes for reduced latency in Asia-Pacific regions
